t1: Truncation solves the size problem, but creates a new one. Stereocilin is a GPI-anchored surface protein: it needs a signal peptide to enter the ER, get glycosylated, receive its GPI anchor, and reach the cell surface. The native signal peptide (residues 1-26) is in the removed N-terminal. No signal peptide = protein stays cytoplasmic = doesn't work.
t2:
Solution: prepend an exogenous signal peptide to the construct. The IgK signal peptide (20 aa, 60 bp) is the most 
          widely used in gene therapy vectors and adds minimal size. The C-terminal GPI-anchor signal (hydrophobic tail + omega site ~S1763) 
          is preserved intact in all constructs.
t3: Full-length stereocilin has 14 predicted N-linked glycosylation sites (NxS/T motifs). Truncation removes sites in the N-terminal:
t4:
Impact unknown. The lost N-terminal glycosylation sites may affect protein folding, ER quality control, 
          or tectorial membrane anchoring. This is the primary experimental risk.
t5: 6. Construct engineering: what goes in the vector
t13: Revised AAV construct map (with IgK signal peptide)
t16: Our codon-optimized CDS contains 156 CpG dinucleotides — 4.9x the human genome average (48.3/kb vs ~10/kb). Unmethylated CpG motifs in AAV transgenes activate TLR9, triggering innate immune responses. In the cochlea, this inflammatory response can damage the very hair cells we're targeting.
t17: All 156 CpGs sit at codon junctions (position 3 + position 1 of adjacent codons). They are 100% eliminable by synonymous codon swaps. Replacing 156 of 1,076 codons (14.5%) reduces CpG from 156 to 21 (87% reduction), drops density to 6.5/kb (below genome average), at a cost of only ~3% CAI loss. GC content drops from 70.5% to 66.3%, also reducing mRNA secondary structure risk.
t18: Top CpG-contributing junctions: P(CCC)-E(GAG) 20x, A(GCC)-A(GCC) 13x, A(GCC)-V(GTG) 9x. These three patterns alone account for 27% of all CpGs.
t19: Action required: CpG-depleted codon re-optimization before any lab experiment. This is a low-cost, high-value safety optimization that should be standard for cochlear AAV constructs.
t21: GC content of 70.5% means 67% of 100-bp windows exceed 70% GC. The 5' region (nt 21-70, encoding the IgK signal peptide C-terminus) has a 73-77% GC hotspot that risks stalling ribosome scanning at the translation initiation site. CpG depletion partially addresses this (GC drops to 66.3%), but the 5' region should be specifically optimized with lower-GC synonymous codons in the first 30-50 codons.
